title: MAINTAINER: Implicit contract between stdin_reader and config -- on_message_hook assumes unencrypted env vars
description: In main.rs, on_message_hook receives [('NIE_FROM', from), ('NIE_TEXT', text)] as env vars. But there's no doc comment explaining this contract, and the values are only available inside the match arm. If someone adds a new hook feature (e.g., retry logic, auth token passing) in Phase 2, they'll have to reverse-engineer what data is available by reading the source. Worse: if the hook needs to access message_id or other fields, there's no invariant doc saying 'NIE_MESSAGE_ID is never populated (Phase 4f)'—maintainers will assume it exists. Fix: doc comment on the struct/function listing all available env vars and which ones are deliberately omitted and why.
close_reason: by design: documented in on_message_hook usage; env vars are the standard IPC mechanism for hook scripts
